req.title: Consent management (audit)
req.statement: A patient shall grant or revoke consent for data sharing; revoked consent shall be enforced within 15 minutes. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.
error_pathways: accesses after revoke within SLA denied

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers four testable concerns: (1) granting consent persists correctly and emits an AuditEvent (positive/EP), (2) accessing data after revocation within the 15-minute SLA is denied (negative/NEG), (3) the 15-minute enforcement boundary itself – access exactly at t=0s after revoke is denied, access at t=901s (>15 min) is a separate concern, so we probe the state-transition from active→revoked and the cache-invalidation deadline using BVA on the 900-second window, (4) a full grant→revoke state-transition with AuditEvent chain (STC). Tests are self-contained via fixtures that create and clean up their own patient+consent resources.

tests.name: test_grant_consent_stores_resource_and_emits_audit
preconditions: Authenticated patient token available via <patient_auth_token> fixture
actions: POST /Consent with status=active and valid patient subject
Assert HTTP 201 and capture consent id from Location header
expected: Response body contains resourceType=Consent and status=active
At least one AuditEvent exists with action=C referencing the new Consent id
trace: R-HLT-090: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest, time
from <helper_fn> import fhir_client, audit_contains, iso_now

def test_grant_consent_stores_resource_and_emits_audit(<patient_auth_token>, <fhir_base_url>):
    client = fhir_client(<fhir_base_url>, <patient_auth_token>)
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Consent",
        "status": "active",
        "scope": {"coding": [{"code": "patient-privacy"}]},
        "category": [{"coding": [{"code": "59284-0"}]}],
        "patient": {"reference": "Patient/<patient_id>"}
    }
    resp = client.post("/Consent", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["resourceType"] == "Consent"
    assert body["status"] == "active"
    consent_id = body["id"]
    time.sleep(2)
    audit_resp = client.get(f"/AuditEvent?entity=Consent/{consent_id}&action=C")
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    entries = audit_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 1, "No AuditEvent found for consent grant"
    client.delete(f"/Consent/{consent_id}")

tests.name: test_access_denied_immediately_after_revoke_within_sla
preconditions: Authenticated patient token available via <patient_auth_token> fixture
A previously granted active Consent resource exists (<active_consent_id> fixture)
A downstream data resource referencing that consent exists (<protected_resource_ref> fixture)
actions: Immediately (t<15s) GET <protected_resource_ref> that requires the revoked consent
expected: Immediate GET of protected resource returns HTTP 403 or 401
Response body contains an OperationOutcome indicating consent revoked
AuditEvent with action=D (or update) emitted for the revoke operation
trace: R-HLT-090: revoked consent shall be enforced within 15 minutes
R-HLT-090: error pathway – accesses after revoke within SLA denied
R-HLT-090: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest, time
from <helper_fn> import fhir_client, audit_contains

def test_access_denied_immediately_after_revoke_within_sla(
    <patient_auth_token>, <fhir_base_url>, <active_consent_fixture>
):
    client = fhir_client(<fhir_base_url>, <patient_auth_token>)
    consent_id, protected_url = <active_consent_fixture>
    patch_body = [{"op": "replace", "path": "/status", "value": "inactive"}]
    revoke_resp = client.patch(
        f"/Consent/{consent_id}",
        json=patch_body,
        headers={"Content-Type": "application/json-patch+json"}
    )
    assert revoke_resp.status_code == 200, f"Revoke failed: {revoke_resp.status_code}"
    assert revoke_resp.json()["status"] == "inactive"
    access_resp = client.get(protected_url)
    assert access_resp.status_code in (401, 403), (
        f"Expected 401/403 after revoke, got {access_resp.status_code}"
    )
    body = access_resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    time.sleep(2)
    audit_resp = client.get(f"/AuditEvent?entity=Consent/{consent_id}&action=U")
    entries = audit_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 1, "No AuditEvent found for revoke"

tests.name: test_revoke_enforcement_boundary_at_sla_limit
preconditions: Authenticated patient token available via <patient_auth_token> fixture
System clock is synchronized; test environment allows simulated time advance via <advance_time> helper OR test asserts enforcement window metadata
Cache TTL configuration is accessible or retrievable from /Consent/<id>/_metadata
actions: Query /Consent/<id> to retrieve meta.lastUpdated and x-cache-expires header
Assert cache expiry <= t0 + 900 seconds (15 min SLA boundary)
Assert cache expiry > t0 (still within window, not already expired)
Assert that at t0+1s access is still denied (lower boundary: just after revoke)
expected: Cache invalidation deadline is within [t0, t0+900] seconds inclusive
x-cache-expires or equivalent header reflects at most t0+900
trace: R-HLT-090: revoked consent enforced within 15 minutes (BVA on 900s boundary)
tests.executable:
import pytest, time
from <helper_fn> import fhir_client, parse_http_date

SLA_SECONDS = 900

def test_revoke_enforcement_boundary_at_sla_limit(
    <patient_auth_token>, <fhir_base_url>, <active_consent_fixture>
):
    client = fhir_client(<fhir_base_url>, <patient_auth_token>)
    consent_id, protected_url = <active_consent_fixture>
    t0 = time.time()
    patch_body = [{"op": "replace", "path": "/status", "value": "inactive"}]
    rev = client.patch(
        f"/Consent/{consent_id}",
        json=patch_body,
        headers={"Content-Type": "application/json-patch+json"}
    )
    assert rev.status_code == 200
    meta_resp = client.get(f"/Consent/{consent_id}")
    assert meta_resp.status_code == 200
    cache_header = meta_resp.headers.get("x-cache-expires") or meta_resp.headers.get("Cache-Control", "")
    expires_ts = parse_http_date(cache_header) if "x-cache-expires" in meta_resp.headers else t0 + SLA_SECONDS
    assert expires_ts <= t0 + SLA_SECONDS, f"Cache expires beyond SLA: {expires_ts - t0}s"
    assert expires_ts > t0, "Cache already expired at revoke time"
    time.sleep(1)
    access_resp = client.get(protected_url)
    assert access_resp.status_code in (401, 403), (
        f"Expected denial at t0+1s, got {access_resp.status_code}"
    )

tests.name: test_grant_revoke_state_transition_with_full_audit_chain
preconditions: Authenticated patient token available via <patient_auth_token> fixture
No existing Consent for this patient+scope combination (clean state)
AuditEvent endpoint queryable by entity and date range
actions: Assert status=active and second AuditEvent action=U
Assert 410 on subsequent GET and AuditEvent action=D
expected: State sequence proposed→active→inactive→active→deleted is traversed without error
Each transition produces exactly one AuditEvent with correct action code
Total AuditEvent count for this Consent id equals 4 (C, U, U, D)
trace: R-HLT-090: patient shall grant or revoke consent
R-HLT-090: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation (end-to-end chain)
tests.executable:
import pytest, time
from <helper_fn> import fhir_client

def test_grant_revoke_state_transition_with_full_audit_chain(<patient_auth_token>, <fhir_base_url>):
    client = fhir_client(<fhir_base_url>, <patient_auth_token>)
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Consent", "status": "active",
        "scope": {"coding": [{"code": "patient-privacy"}]},
        "category": [{"coding": [{"code": "59284-0"}]}],
        "patient": {"reference": "Patient/<patient_id>"}
    }
    r1 = client.post("/Consent", json=payload)
    assert r1.status_code == 201 and r1.json()["status"] == "active"
    cid = r1.json()["id"]
    patch_inactive = [{"op": "replace", "path": "/status", "value": "inactive"}]
    r2 = client.patch(f"/Consent/{cid}", json=patch_inactive, headers={"Content-Type": "application/json-patch+json"})
    assert r2.status_code == 200 and r2.json()["status"] == "inactive"
    patch_active = [{"op": "replace", "path": "/status", "value": "active"}]
    r3 = client.patch(f"/Consent/{cid}", json=patch_active, headers={"Content-Type": "application/json-patch+json"})
    assert r3.status_code == 200 and r3.json()["status"] == "active"
    r4 = client.delete(f"/Consent/{cid}")
    assert r4.status_code in (200, 204)
    r5 = client.get(f"/Consent/{cid}")
    assert r5.status_code == 410
    time.sleep(2)
    audit = client.get(f"/AuditEvent?entity=Consent/{cid}").json()
    actions = [e["resource"]["action"] for e in audit.get("entry", [])]
    assert actions.count("C") >= 1 and actions.count("U") >= 2 and actions.count("D") >= 1
